name: Ermita del Espéritu Santa from Valverde del Fresno — circular the Camino del Espírita Santo
name_original: Ermita del Espéritu Santa desde Valverde del Fresno — circular el Camino del Espírita Santo
region_intro: Hiking around Valverde Del Fresno offers diverse routes through mountainous terrain and historical paths. The region features prominent peaks like Jálama and ancient trails such as the Calzada Romana, providing varied landscapes for exploration. Elevations range from gentle ascents to more challenging climbs, catering to different hiking preferences. The area is characterized by its natural beauty and cultural heritage, making it a compelling destination for outdoor activities. Best hiking trails around Valverde Del Fresno The most popular hiking route is San Martín de Trevejo to Puerto de San Martín via Calzada Romana, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows an ancient Roman road with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Jálama from Puerto de San Martin, a moderate 6.4 miles (10.2 km) path. This trail leads to the Jálama peak, offering expansive views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Ermita del Espéritu Santa from Valverde del Fresno — circular the Camino del Espírita Santo, a 4.1 miles (6.6 km) trail leading through historical paths and gentle hills, often completed in about 1 hour 48 minutes. Hiking around Valverde Del Fresno is defined by mountainous terrain, historical paths, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Valverde Del Fresno are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 150 hikers have used komoot to explore Valverde Del Fresno's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for families in Valverde Del Fresno?
A: Yes, Valverde Del Fresno offers several easy hiking trails. There are 5 routes classified as easy, perfect for families or those seeking a relaxed walk. A good option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1273740212/ermita-del-esperitu-santa-from-valverde-del-fresno-circular-the-camino-del-espirita-santo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Espéritu Santa from Valverde del Fresno — circular the Camino del Espírita Santo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.1 miles (6.6 km) long and takes about 1 hour 48 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes around Valverde Del Fresno?
A: Absolutely. The region features several circular routes, allowing you to start and end your hike in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1272046077/jalama-circular-from-dsa-390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jálama — circular from DSA-390&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 9.7 km loop with scenic views.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or scenic viewpoints to discover on hikes in Valverde Del Fresno?
A: Yes, the area boasts beautiful natural attractions. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2645190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penha Garcia Natural Swimming Pool and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic vistas from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3493856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Erjas River Valley Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4958182" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jálama Summit&lt;/a&gt; also provides expansive views of the surrounding landscape.

Q: Are hiking trails in Valverde Del Fresno dog-friendly?
A: Most hiking trails in Valverde Del Fresno are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. Ensure you carry enough water for both yourself and your pet, particularly during warmer months.

Q: What is the best time of year for hiking in Valverde Del Fresno?
A: The best time for hiking in Valverde Del Fresno is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summer can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon hikes preferable.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Valverde Del Fresno?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Valverde Del Fresno, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse mountainous terrain, the historical paths like the Calzada Romana, and the expansive views from peaks such as Jálama. The variety of routes, from easy to difficult, is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks along the hiking routes?
A: Valverde Del Fresno is rich in history, and several trails pass by significant landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1276816167/san-martin-de-trevejo-to-puerto-de-san-martin-via-calzada-romana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo to Puerto de San Martín via Calzada Romana&lt;/a&gt; route follows an ancient Roman road. You can also visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3555793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Holy Spirit&lt;/a&gt;, a notable religious building in the area.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Valverde Del Fresno?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails documented in Valverde Del Fresno, offering a range of difficulties and lengths. This includes 5 easy routes, 6 moderate routes, and 2 difficult routes, catering to various fitness levels and preferences.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, Valverde Del Fresno offers more challenging routes. The region features 2 difficult trails, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990866880/pico-jalama-loop-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Jálama Loop Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 10.2 km long and involves significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding experience with stunning views from the summit.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Valverde Del Fresno?
A: The terrain around Valverde Del Fresno is predominantly mountainous, featuring a mix of ancient historical paths, forest trails, and open rocky sections, especially as you ascend towards peaks like Jálama. You can expect varied surfaces, from well-trodden paths to more rugged, natural ground.

Q: Is public transport available to reach hiking trailheads?
A: While public transport options within the immediate vicinity of Valverde Del Fresno may be limited, local bus services connect the town with larger regional hubs. For reaching specific trailheads, especially those outside the town center, a car is generally recommended for convenience and flexibility.

Q: Are there places to eat or drink near the hiking trails?
A: Valverde Del Fresno itself offers several local establishments where you can find food and drinks. Some trails may pass through or near small villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6050936" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Martín de Trevejo&lt;/a&gt;, which has amenities. It's always a good idea to carry snacks and water, especially on longer routes, as facilities might not be directly on the trail.

Q: What should I wear for hiking in Valverde Del Fresno?
A: Given the mountainous terrain and varied weather, it's advisable to wear comfortable, layered clothing suitable for hiking. Sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support are recommended. Depending on the season, bring sun protection (hat, sunscreen) and rain gear. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out.
tips: The Hermitage of the Holy Spirit is a chapel located in Eljas. The Hermitage of the Holy Spirit occupies the central nave of what until 1977 was the parish church of Nuestra Señora de la Consolación. When they rebuilt the parish in 1935-1936, they included within their space the site of an old hermitage that, dedicated to the Holy Spirit, dated from the 16th century.
This old bridge marks the beginning of the ascent to Valverde del Fresno. It is not a long or hard climb, but a steady climb until you reach the center.
